title: To Hang Our Head Ostensibly,
content:
To hang our head ostensibly,
    And subsequent to find
    That such was not the posture
    Of our immortal mind,

    Affords the sly presumption
    That, in so dense a fuzz,
    You, too, take cobweb attitudes
    Upon a plane of gauze!
meaning:
Emily Elizabeth Dickinson's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "To Hang Our Head Ostensibly,"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
